seoTitle: How to Create a Microsoft Loop Template (2026)
metaDescription: Turn any formatted Microsoft Loop page into a reusable template you can save to your team's gallery and deploy with one click.
intro: If you find yourself rebuilding the same structure across multiple Loop pages, saving it as a template removes the repetitive work. This guide walks through formatting a page the way you want your team to reuse it, saving it as a custom template, and confirming it appears correctly for future pages.

steps.heading: Open your working directory
steps.body: Open your Microsoft Loop app and select the workspace where you want the new template available.

steps.heading: Build your structure
steps.body: Create a new page and build it out using tables, task lists, and content blocks exactly as you want your team to see them.

steps.heading: Access the template tools
steps.body: Move to the top right page options, click the three dots icon, and locate the template action tools.

steps.heading: Save as a template
steps.body: Select 'Save as Template' from the dropdown menu to open the configuration drawer.

steps.heading: Name and categorize it
steps.body: Enter a descriptive name, assign a target category or workspace, and click Save to lock it into your shared team gallery.

steps.heading: Test the pipeline
steps.body: Spawn a brand new page and confirm your custom template appears ready for one-click deployment.

faq.question: Where do saved templates show up for teammates?
faq.answer: They're saved into your shared team gallery, where anyone creating a new page can select them.

faq.question: Can I assign a template to a specific category?
faq.answer: Yes, the save dialog lets you assign a category or workspace so the template is easier to find later.

faq.question: Does creating a template duplicate the original page's data?
faq.answer: The templating process converts the page structure into a reusable blueprint without maintaining a live link back to the original page's data.

faq.question: Can I have multiple templates for different types of documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, you can save as many templates as your team needs, each with its own name and category.
